attacking_style: Consistent breakaway rider who targets early moves rather than late-race attacks. Demonstrates willingness to commit to consecutive days in the break, suggesting endurance-based opportunism rather than explosive climbing attacks.
team_tactics: Used as a positioning tool for intermediate sprint competitions and general classification support rather than sprint train development. Functions as an expendable breakaway asset to gain team visibility and bonus points.
terrain_preference: Suited to long breakaways on transitional stages where sustained effort matters more than explosive power. Avoids scenarios requiring final-kilometer punch or steep gradient attacks.
vulnerability: Lacks finishing speed to capitalize on breakaway efforts - repeatedly present in breaks but unable to convert. Veterans status suggests susceptibility to sustained high-pace racing and multiple climbing efforts.
key_patterns: Consecutive day breakaway presence across three separate stage races (Tour de Romandie, Suisse, Guangxi)
Deployed for intermediate sprint positioning rather than final result objectives across team changes
Veteran status maintaining aggressive racing form but without conversion to results in 2018
form_trajectory: neutral: Shows consistent willingness to race aggressively throughout 2018 season, but lack of result conversion suggests stable but limited form rather than progression
